sections.title: Which camp is correct? How is lift generated?
How do engineers determine the weight of an airplane which they are designing?
OPTIONS: Aircraft Type, Problem Type, Answer Type, Program Mode, Units
Control Panel Choices: Mode, Units, Output Display
Lifting airfoils are designed to have the upper surface longer than the bottom.
Air molecules travel faster over the top to meet molecules moving underneath at the trailing edge.
The upper flow is faster and from Bernoulli’s equation the pressure is lower.
Lift and Drag of a Glider
So how does a glider generate the velocity needed for flight?
How do gliders stay aloft for hours if they constantly descend?
Effect of Velocity on Aerodynamic Forces
Effect of Air Density on Aerodynamic Forces
What are Newton’s Laws of Motion?
An object at rest remains at rest, and an object in motion remains in motion at constant speed and in a straight line unless acted on by an unbalanced force.
The acceleration of an object depends on the mass of the object and the amount of force applied.
Whenever one object exerts a force on a second object, the second object exerts an equal and opposite force on the first.
